title: Within-lap LambdaRank specialist over the active risk set
summary: At each lap, only a few cars are truly near a stop, so relative urgency inside the same race-lap may matter more than absolute probability.
rationale: At each lap, only a few cars are truly near a stop, so relative urgency inside the same race-lap may matter more than absolute probability. LambdaRank is built for grouped ranking problems and can learn who should be highest-risk within each query, which matches the strategist decision frame better than global classification alone.
implementation_hint: Create ranker queries as `(Year, Race, LapNumber)` or `(Year, Race, LapNumber, dry/wet regime)`, label pitters on the next lap as relevant, train `LGBMRanker` with causal features only, and blend its normalized scores with the existing classifier. Evaluate both pure rank AUC and blended AUC.
expected_effect: Can improve ordering among contemporaneous candidates, which is exactly what ROC AUC rewards when many negatives are easy and only local comparisons are hard.
risk: Some laps may have zero or multiple positives, and ranker outputs need careful scaling before blending into probability space.
